description_long: This object is a compact solar energy module, likely a photovoltaic (PV) panel with an integrated heat sink or cooling fin structure on its upper surface. The main body consists of a rectangular, flat panel with a metallic grid or ribbed structure, suggesting efficient heat dissipation. The base features a yellow circuit board with visible electronic components, connectors, and a gold-plated edge, indicating it is designed for integration into a larger electrical system, possibly as a solar power supply for urban infrastructure. The side profile reveals the layered construction, with the heat sink rising above the circuit board. The overall design is robust and engineered for efficient energy conversion and thermal management. Its size and form factor suggest it is intended for installation in compact urban environments, such as on street furniture, small rooftops, or integrated into smart city devices.
description: A compact solar panel module with a ribbed metallic heat sink on top and a yellow circuit board base, designed for efficient energy conversion and integration into urban infrastructure.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full length of the panel with the ribbed heat sink structure prominently on top and the yellow circuit board base visible below.
description_view_1: The side view highlights the thickness of the heat sink and the layered construction, with electronic components visible along the edge.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the flat back of the panel and the extended gold-plated connector, emphasizing the module's integration features.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the heat sink fins and additional electronic components, mirroring the details seen in view 1.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: circuit board-mounted with aluminum heat sink frame
